# osdgoogle Provider

The osdgoogle provider manages Red Hat OpenShift Dedicated (OSD) clusters on Google Cloud Platform via the OpenShift Cluster Manager (OCM) API. It supports creating clusters with Workload Identity Federation (WIF), machine pools, and identity providers.

## Project status

This provider is **experimental** software built and maintained by the **Red Hat Managed OpenShift Black Belt** team. It is **not** a supported Red Hat product and **should not be considered production-ready**. Use at your own risk; behavior and APIs may change without notice.

## Introduction

### Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager

Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager is a managed service where you can install, modify, operate, and upgrade your Red Hat OpenShift clusters. This service allows you to work with all of your organization's clusters from a single dashboard.

More information can be found [here](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_openshift_cluster_manager/).

### OSD on GCP

OpenShift Dedicated on GCP uses Workload Identity Federation to provide secure, keyless authentication between OCM and your GCP project. The provider and its modules handle creating the WIF configuration in OCM, provisioning the corresponding GCP IAM resources, and deploying the cluster.

### Terraform

Terraform is an infrastructure-as-code tool that allows you to define resources in human-readable configuration files. For more information, see the [Terraform documentation](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform).

## Prerequisites

To use the osdgoogle provider inside your Terraform configuration you must meet the following:

* **OCM token** — An offline [OCM token](https://console.redhat.com/openshift/token/rosa) from the Red Hat Hybrid Cloud Console. This token verifies your access and permission to create and manage clusters. The token is unique to your account and should not be shared.

* **GCP project** — A Google Cloud project with billing enabled and the required APIs enabled for OSD on GCP.

* **GCP credentials** — Application Default Credentials configured (e.g., `gcloud auth application-default login`). The identity used must have the [minimum GCP IAM permissions](#minimum-gcp-iam-permissions) listed below.

* **Terraform** — [Terraform](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/downloads) version 1.0 or newer.

## Minimum GCP IAM Permissions

The following table lists the minimum GCP IAM roles required for the identity running Terraform. These permissions apply to the GCP project where clusters and WIF resources are created.

| Component | Minimum Roles | Purpose |
|-----------|----------------|---------|
| **osd-wif-gcp** | `roles/iam.workloadIdentityPoolAdmin`, `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin`, `roles/iam.roleAdmin`, `roles/resourcemanager.projectIamAdmin` | Create workload identity pool, OIDC provider, service accounts, custom roles, and IAM bindings |
| **osd-vpc** (without PSC) | `roles/compute.networkAdmin`, `roles/compute.routerAdmin` | Create VPC, subnets, Cloud Router, Cloud NAT, firewall rules |
| **osd-vpc** (with PSC) | Above + `roles/dns.admin` | Additional Private DNS zones for Private Service Connect |
| **osd-wif-config** | `roles/resourcemanager.projectViewer` | Read project number for OCM (or any role with `resourcemanager.projects.get`) |

For a simpler setup, `roles/editor` covers compute and DNS, and `roles/owner` covers IAM. However, `roles/iam.workloadIdentityPoolAdmin` is not included in Editor or Owner and must be granted separately for WIF.

## Authentication and Configuration

Authentication to OCM is done via an offline access token or client credentials. Configuration can be derived from:

1. Parameters in the provider configuration
2. Environment variables

### Provider Configuration

!> **Warning:** Hard-coded credentials are not recommended in any Terraform configuration and risk secret leakage should this file ever be committed to a public version control system.

The OCM token can be provided in the provider block:

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    osdgoogle = {
      source  = "rh-mobb/osd-google"
      version = ">= 0.1.0"
    }
    google = {
      source  = "hashicorp/google"
      version = ">= 5.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "osdgoogle" {
  token             = var.ocm_token
  openshift_version = "4.21.3"
}

provider "google" {
  project = var.gcp_project_id
}
```

Alternatively, use environment variables and leave sensitive attributes unset:

```terraform
provider "osdgoogle" {}
```

```console
$ export OSDGOOGLE_TOKEN="your-offline-token"
```

Supported environment variables: `OSDGOOGLE_TOKEN`, `OSDGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `OSDGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `OSDGOOGLE_URL`, `OSDGOOGLE_TOKEN_URL`, `OSDGOOGLE_TRUSTED_CAS`.

## Getting Started

Choose a deployment path based on your networking requirements:

* [Deploy a basic cluster](guides/deploy-basic-cluster.md) — Use default networking with the `osd-wif-config` and `osd-cluster` modules.
* [Deploy a BYO VPC cluster](guides/deploy-byo-vpc-cluster.md) — Bring your own VPC using the `osd-vpc`, `osd-wif-config`, and `osd-cluster` modules, with optional Private Service Connect (PSC).

## Example Terraform configurations

Example configurations are available in the [terraform-provider-osd-google](https://github.com/rh-mobb/terraform-provider-osd-google) repository:

* [Basic cluster](https://github.com/rh-mobb/terraform-provider-osd-google/tree/main/examples/cluster)
* [BYO VPC cluster](https://github.com/rh-mobb/terraform-provider-osd-google/tree/main/examples/cluster_with_vpc)
* [Shared VPC cluster](https://github.com/rh-mobb/terraform-provider-osd-google/tree/main/examples/cluster_shared_vpc)
* [Multi-AZ cluster](https://github.com/rh-mobb/terraform-provider-osd-google/tree/main/examples/cluster_multi_az)
* [Cluster with PSC](https://github.com/rh-mobb/terraform-provider-osd-google/tree/main/examples/cluster_psc)

## Schema

### Optional

- `client_id` (String) OpenID client identifier for client credentials authentication (alternative to token). Use with client_secret. Env: OSDGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) OpenID client secret for client credentials authentication. Env: OSDGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.
- `insecure` (Boolean) When set to 'true' enables insecure communication with the server. This disables verification of TLS certificates and host names. Not recommended for production.
- `openshift_version` (String) Default OpenShift version (x.y.z) for cluster and WIF resources. Overridable per resource.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) Access or refresh token generated from https://console.redhat.com/openshift/token/rosa
- `token_url` (String) TokenURL is the URL for requesting OpenID access tokens. The default is `https://sso.redhat.com/auth/realms/redhat-external/protocol/openid-connect/token`
- `trusted_cas` (String) PEM encoded certificates of authorities that will be trusted. If not specified, the system default CAs are used.
- `url` (String) URL sets the base URL of the API gateway. The default is `https://api.openshift.com`
